96	    def __init__(
97	        self,
98	        disable_tool_calls: bool = False,
99	        trajectory_config: TrajectoryConfig | None = None,
100	        *args,
101	        **kwargs,
102	    ):
103	        """
104	        Initialize OpenHands agent.
105	
106	        Args:
107	            disable_tool_calls: Whether to disable tool calls.
108	                If true, native function calling will be disabled.
109	            trajectory_config: Optional TrajectoryConfig containing trajectory-related configurations.
110	                Available options:
111	                - raw_content (bool): If True, read trajectory from raw completions folder instead
112	                  of parsed event logs. Useful for preserving raw LLM responses. (default: False)
113	        """
114	        # Extract api_base and model_info before passing kwargs to super()
115	        self._api_base = kwargs.pop("api_base", None)
116	        self._model_info = kwargs.pop("model_info", None)
117	        raw_python_version = kwargs.pop("python_version", "3.13")
118	        self._python_version = str(raw_python_version)
119	        super().__init__(disable_tool_calls=disable_tool_calls, *args, **kwargs)
120	        if isinstance(raw_python_version, (int, float)):
121	            self.logger.debug(
122	                "python_version=%r parsed as %s — quote it in YAML to avoid "
123	                'issues with versions like 3.10: python_version: "%s"',
124	                raw_python_version,
125	                type(raw_python_version).__name__,
126	                raw_python_version,
127	            )
128	        self._disable_tool_calls = disable_tool_calls
129	        self._git_version = kwargs.get("git_version", None)
130	        self._trajectory_config = trajectory_config or {}
131	        self._save_raw_content_in_trajectory = self._trajectory_config.get(
132	            "raw_content", False
133	        )
134	
135	        # Validate that raw_content requires disable_tool_calls
136	        if self._save_raw_content_in_trajectory and not self._disable_tool_calls:
137	            raise ValueError(
138	                "When trajectory_config['raw_content'] is True, disable_tool_calls must also be True. "
139	                "Raw content mode is designed to preserve text-based tool invocation format (e.g., <execute_bash>) "
140	                "rather than native function calling format. Native function calling does not appear in LLM response's "
141	                "message content field, rather, it is structured in the tool_calls field, thus there is no easy and "
142	                "standard way to persist it in the trajectory in its raw/original format. Note that even OpenHands may not "
143	                "use native function calling for some models, disable_tool_calls must still be set explicitly in raw_content mode."
144	            )
145	
146	    @staticmethod
147	    @override
148	    def name() -> str:
149	        return AgentName.OPENHANDS.value
150	
151	    @property
152	    def _trajectory_path(self) -> PurePosixPath:
153	        return PurePosixPath(EnvironmentPaths.agent_dir / "trajectory.json")
154	
155	    def _get_session_dir(self) -> Path | None:
156	        """Get the single session directory."""
157	        sessions_dir = self.logs_dir / "sessions"
158	        if not sessions_dir.exists():
159	            return None
160	
161	        session_dirs = [d for d in sessions_dir.iterdir() if d.is_dir()]
162	        if not session_dirs:
163	            return None
164	
165	        # Sanity check: there should be exactly one session
166	        assert len(session_dirs) == 1, (
167	            f"Expected exactly 1 session, found {len(session_dirs)}"
168	        )
169	        return session_dirs[0]
170	
171	    def _extract_version_and_metadata(
172	        self, events_dir: Path, include_tool_definitions: bool = True
173	    ) -> tuple[str, dict[str, Any] | None, list[dict[str, Any]] | None]:
174	        """Extract version, extra metadata, and optionally tool definitions from event files.
175	
176	        Args:
177	            events_dir: Directory containing event files
178	            include_tool_definitions: Whether to extract tool definitions (default: True)
179	
180	        Returns:
181	            Tuple of (version, extra, tool_definitions)
182	        """
183	        version = "unknown"
184	        extra: dict[str, Any] | None = None
185	        tool_definitions: list[dict[str, Any]] | None = None
186	
187	        event_files = sorted(events_dir.glob("*.json"), key=lambda p: int(p.stem))
188	        for event_file in event_files:
189	            try:
190	                with open(event_file, "r") as f:
191	                    event = json.load(f)
192	                    if "args" in event:
193	                        if "openhands_version" in event["args"]:
194	                            version = event["args"]["openhands_version"]
195	
196	                        # Extract extra info
197	                        extra_data = {
198	                            key: event["args"][key]
199	                            for key in ["agent_class"]
200	                            if key in event["args"]
201	                        }
202	                        if extra_data:
203	                            extra = extra_data
204	
205	                        # Extract tool definitions if requested and available
206	                        if include_tool_definitions and "tools" in event["args"]:
207	                            tool_definitions = event["args"]["tools"]
208	
209	                        if version != "unknown":
210	                            break
211	            except Exception as e:
212	                self.logger.debug(f"Could not read event file {event_file}: {e}")
213	
214	        return version, extra, tool_definitions
215	
216	    def _convert_event_to_step(self, event: dict[str, Any], step_id: int) -> Step:
217	        """Convert an OpenHands event to ATIF trajectory step format using Pydantic models."""
218	        # Map OpenHands source to ATIF-compliant source
219	        source = event.get("source", "")
220	        message = event.get("message", "")
221	
222	        # Determine if this is a system message based on various heuristics
223	        # Check if this is a system prompt (action="system" regardless of source)
224	        if event.get("action") == "system":
225	            source = "system"
226	        elif source == "environment":
227	            # Map environment to system as per ATIF spec
228	            source = "system"
229	        elif source == "user" and message:
230	            # Heuristic: System-generated messages often start with specific patterns
231	            # These are internal OpenHands status messages, not actual user input
232	            system_message_patterns = [
233	                "Retrieving content for:",
234	                "Added workspace context",
235	                "Loading workspace",
236	                "Initializing",
237	            ]
238	            if any(message.startswith(pattern) for pattern in system_message_patterns):
239	                source = "system"
240	
241	        # Extract timestamp
242	        timestamp = event.get("timestamp", "")
243	
244	        # Handle tool calls for agent actions (only for agent source)
245	        tool_calls: list[ToolCall] | None = None
246	        if event.get("source") == "agent" and "tool_call_metadata" in event:
247	            metadata = event["tool_call_metadata"]
248	            arguments: dict[str, Any] = {}
249	
250	            # Extract arguments if available from model_response
251	            if "model_response" in metadata:
252	                model_resp = metadata["model_response"]
253	                if "choices" in model_resp and len(model_resp["choices"]) > 0:
254	                    choice = model_resp["choices"][0]
255	                    if "message" in choice and "tool_calls" in choice["message"]:
256	                        tc_list = choice["message"]["tool_calls"]
257	                        if tc_list and len(tc_list) > 0:
258	                            if (
259	                                "function" in tc_list[0]
260	                                and "arguments" in tc_list[0]["function"]
261	                            ):
262	                                try:
263	                                    # Parse arguments if they're a JSON string
264	                                    args = tc_list[0]["function"]["arguments"]
265	                                    if isinstance(args, str):
266	                                        arguments = json.loads(args)
267	                                    else:
268	                                        arguments = args
269	                                except (json.JSONDecodeError, KeyError):
270	                                    arguments = {}
271	
272	            tool_calls = [
273	                ToolCall(
274	                    tool_call_id=metadata.get("tool_call_id", ""),
275	                    function_name=metadata.get("function_name", ""),
276	                    arguments=arguments,
277	                )
278	            ]
279	
280	        # Handle observations (tool call results) - only for agent source
281	        observation: Observation | None = None
282	        if (
283	            "observation" in event
284	            and event.get("cause") is not None
285	            and event.get("source") == "agent"
286	        ):
287	            # Link observation to tool call if available
288	            source_call_id = None
289	            if "tool_call_metadata" in event:
290	                source_call_id = event["tool_call_metadata"].get("tool_call_id")
291	
292	            # Note: We're not storing OpenHands-specific metadata (type, extras, success)
293	            # in the observation since the current Pydantic model doesn't have an extra field.
294	            # This could be added in the future if needed.
295	            observation = Observation(
296	                results=[
297	                    ObservationResult(
298	                        source_call_id=source_call_id,
299	                        content=event.get("content", ""),
300	                    )
301	                ]
302	            )
303	
304	        # Add metrics if available (only for agent source)
305	        metrics: Metrics | None = None
306	        if "llm_metrics" in event and event.get("source") == "agent":
307	            llm_metrics = event["llm_metrics"]
308	            accumulated_usage = llm_metrics.get("accumulated_token_usage", {})
309	            prompt_tokens = accumulated_usage.get("prompt_tokens", 0)
310	            completion_tokens = accumulated_usage.get("completion_tokens", 0)
311	            cached_tokens = accumulated_usage.get("cache_read_tokens", 0)
312	            cost_usd = llm_metrics.get("accumulated_cost", 0)
313	
314	            metrics = Metrics(
315	                prompt_tokens=prompt_tokens if prompt_tokens > 0 else None,
316	                completion_tokens=completion_tokens if completion_tokens > 0 else None,
317	                cached_tokens=cached_tokens if cached_tokens > 0 else None,
318	                cost_usd=cost_usd if cost_usd > 0 else None,
319	            )
320	
321	        return Step(
322	            step_id=step_id,
323	            timestamp=timestamp,
324	            source=source,
325	            message=message,
326	            tool_calls=tool_calls,
327	            observation=observation,
328	            metrics=metrics,
329	        )
330	
331	    def _convert_events_to_trajectory(self, events_dir: Path) -> Trajectory | None:
332	        """
333	        Convert OpenHands event files to ATIF trajectory format.
334	
335	        This method reads from the events folder which contains structured tool calls
336	        no matter if function calling is enabled or disabled for that model.
337	
338	        Args:
339	            events_dir: Directory containing event files
340	
341	        Returns:
342	            Trajectory object with parsed tool calls
343	
344	        Raises:
345	            ValueError: If events directory doesn't exist or contains no event files
346	        """
347	        # Read all event files
348	        event_files = sorted(events_dir.glob("*.json"), key=lambda p: int(p.stem))
349	        events = []
350	        for event_file in event_files:
351	            try:
352	                with open(event_file, "r") as f:
353	                    events.append(json.load(f))
354	            except Exception as e:
355	                self.logger.debug(f"Could not read event file {event_file}: {e}")
356	
357	        if not events:
358	            return None
359	
360	        # Extract version and metadata from events
361	        version, extra, tool_definitions = self._extract_version_and_metadata(
362	            events_dir
363	        )
364	
365	        # Generate session_id from events directory path
366	        # Use the parent directory name (session folder name) as session_id
367	        session_id = events_dir.parent.name
368	
369	        # Convert events to steps (step_id starts from 1 per ATIF spec)
370	        # Note: OpenHands stores accumulated metrics in each event, but ATIF expects
371	        # per-step deltas. We'll need to calculate deltas after conversion.
372	        steps: list[Step] = []
373	        step_counter = 1
374	        for event in events:
375	            step = self._convert_event_to_step(event, step_counter)
376	
377	            # OpenHands trajectories contain bookkeeping system steps
378	            # that don't contain meaningful information as trajectory steps.
379	            if step.message or step.tool_calls or step.observation:
380	                # Check if this step should be merged with the previous step
381	                # OpenHands sometimes emits two events for the same action:
382	                # 1. Agent action with tool_call but no observation
383	                # 2. Same agent action with observation (sometimes repeating the tool_call)
384	                # We merge these into a single step by checking if the tool_call_id matches
385	                should_merge = False
386	                if (
387	                    steps
388	                    and step.source == "agent"
389	                    and step.observation
390	                    and step.tool_calls
391	                    and steps[-1].source == "agent"
392	                    and steps[-1].tool_calls
393	                    and not steps[-1].observation
394	                ):
395	                    # Check if the tool_call_ids match
396	                    prev_call_id = steps[-1].tool_calls[0].tool_call_id
397	                    curr_call_id = step.tool_calls[0].tool_call_id
398	                    if prev_call_id == curr_call_id:
399	                        should_merge = True
400	
401	                if should_merge:
402	                    # Merge this step into the previous step
403	                    # Add observation to previous step and preserve message if present
404	                    steps[-1].observation = step.observation
405	                    if step.message and not steps[-1].message:
406	                        steps[-1].message = step.message
407	                    # Don't increment step_counter since we're merging
408	                else:
409	                    steps.append(step)
410	                    step_counter += 1
411	
412	        # Convert accumulated metrics to per-step deltas
413	        # OpenHands events contain accumulated_token_usage, but ATIF steps should
414	        # contain per-step token usage. Calculate deltas between consecutive steps.
415	        prev_prompt = 0
416	        prev_completion = 0
417	        prev_cached = 0
418	        prev_cost = 0.0
419	
420	        for step in steps:
421	            if step.metrics:
422	                # Get current accumulated values
423	                curr_prompt = step.metrics.prompt_tokens or 0
424	                curr_completion = step.metrics.completion_tokens or 0
425	                curr_cached = step.metrics.cached_tokens or 0
426	                curr_cost = step.metrics.cost_usd or 0.0
427	
428	                # Calculate delta (this step's usage only)
429	                delta_prompt = curr_prompt - prev_prompt
430	                delta_completion = curr_completion - prev_completion
431	                delta_cached = curr_cached - prev_cached
432	                delta_cost = curr_cost - prev_cost
433	
434	                # Update step metrics with deltas
435	                step.metrics.prompt_tokens = delta_prompt if delta_prompt > 0 else None
436	                step.metrics.completion_tokens = (
437	                    delta_completion if delta_completion > 0 else None
438	                )
439	                step.metrics.cached_tokens = delta_cached if delta_cached > 0 else None
440	                step.metrics.cost_usd = delta_cost if delta_cost > 0 else None
441	
442	                # Update previous values for next iteration
443	                prev_prompt = curr_prompt
444	                prev_completion = curr_completion
445	                prev_cached = curr_cached
446	                prev_cost = curr_cost
447	
448	        # Extract final metrics from last step with metrics
449	        final_metrics: FinalMetrics | None = None
450	        for event in reversed(events):
451	            if "llm_metrics" in event:
452	                accumulated_usage = event["llm_metrics"].get(
453	                    "accumulated_token_usage", {}
454	                )
455	                total_prompt_tokens = accumulated_usage.get("prompt_tokens", 0)
456	                total_completion_tokens = accumulated_usage.get("completion_tokens", 0)
457	                total_cached_tokens = accumulated_usage.get("cache_read_tokens", 0)
458	                total_cost_usd = event["llm_metrics"].get("accumulated_cost", 0)
459	
460	                final_metrics = FinalMetrics(
461	                    total_prompt_tokens=total_prompt_tokens
462	                    if total_prompt_tokens > 0
463	                    else None,
464	                    total_completion_tokens=total_completion_tokens
465	                    if total_completion_tokens > 0
466	                    else None,
467	                    total_cached_tokens=total_cached_tokens
468	                    if total_cached_tokens > 0
469	                    else None,
470	                    total_cost_usd=total_cost_usd if total_cost_usd > 0 else None,
471	                )
472	                break
473	
474	        # Build trajectory using Pydantic models
475	        trajectory = Trajectory(
476	            schema_version="ATIF-v1.5",
477	            session_id=session_id,
478	            agent=Agent(
479	                name="openhands",
480	                version=version,
481	                tool_definitions=tool_definitions,
482	                extra=extra,
483	            ),
484	            steps=steps,
485	            final_metrics=final_metrics,
486	        )
487	
488	        return trajectory
489	
490	    def _convert_completions_to_trajectory(
491	        self, completions_dir: Path, events_dir: Path
492	    ) -> Trajectory | None:
493	        """Convert OpenHands completion files to ATIF trajectory format with raw LLM responses.
494	
495	        This method reads from the completions folder which preserves the original format of LLM responses
496	        when function calling is disabled or not available for that model.
497	
498	        Args:
499	            completions_dir: Directory containing completion JSON files
500	            events_dir: Directory containing event files (used to extract tool definitions when needed)
501	
502	        Returns:
503	            Trajectory object, or None if completions are not available
504	        """
505	        if not completions_dir.exists():
506	            self.logger.debug(
507	                f"Completions directory does not exist: {completions_dir}. "
508	                "Cannot generate trajectory when raw_content=True."
509	            )
510	            return None
511	
512	        # Read all completion files sorted by timestamp in filename
513	        # Filename format: provider__model-name-timestamp.json
514	        # Example: anthropic__claude-sonnet-4-5-1765823587.968077.json
515	        def get_timestamp(path: Path) -> float:
516	            """Extract timestamp from completion filename."""
517	            try:
518	                # Split by '-' and get the last part before .json
519	                parts = path.stem.split("-")
520	                # The timestamp is typically at the end: timestamp1.timestamp2
521	                timestamp_str = parts[-1]
522	                return float(timestamp_str)
523	            except (ValueError, IndexError):
524	                return 0.0
525	
526	        completion_files = sorted(
527	            completions_dir.glob("*.json"),
528	            key=get_timestamp,
529	        )
530	
531	        if not completion_files:
532	            self.logger.debug(
533	                f"No completion files found in {completions_dir}. "
534	                "Cannot generate trajectory when raw_content=True."
535	            )
536	            return None
537	
538	        # Extract session info from first completion
539	        try:
540	            with open(completion_files[0], "r") as f:
541	                first_completion = json.load(f)
542	        except Exception as e:
543	            self.logger.debug(f"Could not read first completion file: {e}")
544	            return None
545	
546	        # Determine if using native function calling by checking if tools are in kwargs
547	        using_native_function_calling = "tools" in first_completion.get("kwargs", {})
548	
549	        # Extract version and metadata from events
550	        # Only include tool_definitions when using native function calling
551	        # When using text-based tool invocation (disable_tool_calls=True, or
552	        # this model does not support function calling, or OpenHands wrongly assumes
553	        # it does not support function calling), the tools are already defined inline
554	        # in the system prompt
555	        version, extra, tool_definitions = self._extract_version_and_metadata(
556	            events_dir, include_tool_definitions=using_native_function_calling
557	        )
558	
559	        # Generate session_id from completions directory path
560	        session_id = completions_dir.parent.parent.name
561	
562	        # Build steps from completions
563	        steps: list[Step] = []
564	        step_counter = 1
565	        total_prompt_tokens = 0
566	        total_completion_tokens = 0
567	        total_cached_tokens = 0
568	        total_cost_usd = 0.0
569	
570	        for completion_idx, completion_file in enumerate(completion_files):
571	            try:
572	                with open(completion_file, "r") as f:
573	                    completion = json.load(f)
574	            except Exception as e:
575	                self.logger.debug(
576	                    f"Could not read completion file {completion_file}: {e}"
577	                )
578	                continue
579	
580	            messages = completion.get("messages", [])
581	            fncall_messages = completion.get("fncall_messages", [])
582	            response = completion.get("response", {})
583	            timestamp_float = completion.get("timestamp", 0)
584	
585	            # Convert timestamp to ISO format
586	            from datetime import datetime, timezone
587	
588	            timestamp = datetime.fromtimestamp(
589	                timestamp_float, tz=timezone.utc
590	            ).isoformat()
591	
592	            # For the FIRST completion: extract system and user messages from messages array
593	            # For SUBSEQUENT completions: extract observation from fncall_messages
594	            if completion_idx == 0:
595	                # First completion: process system and user messages
596	                for msg in messages:
597	                    role = msg.get("role", "")
598	                    if role == "system":
599	                        # Add system message as first step
600	                        content_parts = msg.get("content", [])
601	                        if isinstance(content_parts, list) and len(content_parts) > 0:
602	                            system_content = content_parts[0].get("text", "")
603	                            steps.append(
604	                                Step(
605	                                    step_id=step_counter,
606	                                    timestamp=timestamp,
607	                                    source="system",
608	                                    message=system_content,
609	                                )
610	                            )
611	                            step_counter += 1
612	                    elif role == "user":
613	                        # Add user message
614	                        content_parts = msg.get("content", [])
615	                        if isinstance(content_parts, list) and len(content_parts) > 0:
616	                            user_content = content_parts[0].get("text", "")
617	                            steps.append(
618	                                Step(
619	                                    step_id=step_counter,
620	                                    timestamp=timestamp,
621	                                    source="user",
622	                                    message=user_content,
623	                                )
624	                            )
625	                            step_counter += 1
626	            else:
627	                # Subsequent completions: extract observation from fncall_messages
628	                # The observation is the last "tool" role message in fncall_messages
629	                for msg in reversed(fncall_messages):
630	                    if msg.get("role") == "tool":
631	                        # Extract observation content
632	                        content_data = msg.get("content", [])
633	                        if isinstance(content_data, list) and len(content_data) > 0:
634	                            observation_content = content_data[0].get("text", "")
635	                        elif isinstance(content_data, str):
636	                            observation_content = content_data
637	                        else:
638	                            observation_content = ""
639	
640	                        # Attach observation to the previous agent step
641	                        # In text-based tool invocation mode, we don't have structured tool_calls,
642	                        # so we don't set source_call_id (leaving it as None is valid per ATIF spec)
643	                        if steps and steps[-1].source == "agent":
644	                            steps[-1].observation = Observation(
645	                                results=[
646	                                    ObservationResult(
647	                                        source_call_id=None,
648	                                        content=observation_content,
649	                                    )
650	                                ]
651	                            )
652	                        break
653	
654	            # Process response - extract raw content and metrics
655	            choices = response.get("choices", [])
656	            if not choices:
657	                continue
658	
659	            message = choices[0].get("message", {})
660	            content = message.get("content") or ""
661	
662	            # Extract usage metrics
663	            usage = response.get("usage", {})
664	            prompt_tokens = usage.get("prompt_tokens", 0)
665	            completion_tokens = usage.get("completion_tokens", 0)
666	            # Handle different cache token field names
667	            cached_tokens = usage.get("cache_read_tokens", 0) or usage.get(
668	                "cache_read_input_tokens", 0
669	            )
670	
671	            # Get cost from completion metadata
672	            cost = completion.get("cost", 0.0)
673	
674	            # Update totals
675	            total_prompt_tokens += prompt_tokens
676	            total_completion_tokens += completion_tokens
677	            total_cached_tokens += cached_tokens
678	            total_cost_usd += cost
679	
680	            # Create metrics object
681	            metrics = Metrics(
682	                prompt_tokens=prompt_tokens if prompt_tokens > 0 else None,
683	                completion_tokens=completion_tokens if completion_tokens > 0 else None,
684	                cached_tokens=cached_tokens if cached_tokens > 0 else None,
685	                cost_usd=cost if cost > 0 else None,
686	            )
687	
688	            # Add agent response step with raw content (no tool_calls parsing)
689	            steps.append(
690	                Step(
691	                    step_id=step_counter,
692	                    timestamp=timestamp,
693	                    source="agent",
694	                    message=content,
695	                    metrics=metrics,
696	                )
697	            )
698	            step_counter += 1
699	
700	        # Build final metrics
701	        final_metrics = FinalMetrics(
702	            total_prompt_tokens=total_prompt_tokens
703	            if total_prompt_tokens > 0
704	            else None,
705	            total_completion_tokens=total_completion_tokens
706	            if total_completion_tokens > 0
707	            else None,
708	            total_cached_tokens=total_cached_tokens
709	            if total_cached_tokens > 0
710	            else None,
711	            total_cost_usd=total_cost_usd if total_cost_usd > 0 else None,
712	        )
713	
714	        # Build trajectory
715	        trajectory = Trajectory(
716	            schema_version="ATIF-v1.5",
717	            session_id=session_id,
718	            agent=Agent(
719	                name="openhands",
720	                version=version,
721	                tool_definitions=tool_definitions,
722	                extra=extra,
723	            ),
724	            steps=steps,
725	            final_metrics=final_metrics,
726	        )
727	
728	        return trajectory
729	
730	    @override
731	    def populate_context_post_run(self, context: AgentContext) -> None:
732	        """
733	        Populate context after agent run completes or times out.
734	
735	        This method:
736	        1. Gets the session directory
737	        2. Converts individual event/completion files to a single trajectory.json file
738	        3. Populates context with token usage and cost information
739	
740	        When raw_content=True in trajectory_config:
741	        - Reads from agent/completions folder which preserves raw LLM responses
742	        - Requires completions to be available (raises error if not)
743	
744	        When raw_content=False (default):
745	        - Reads from events folder which contains parsed tool calls
746	        """
747	        # Get the session directory
748	        session_dir = self._get_session_dir()
749	        if not session_dir:
750	            self.logger.debug("No session directory found")
751	            return
752	
753	        events_dir = session_dir / "events"
754	        if not events_dir.exists():
755	            self.logger.debug(f"Events directory {events_dir} does not exist")
756	            return
757	
758	        # Convert to trajectory based on raw_content setting
759	        trajectory: Trajectory | None = None
760	        if self._save_raw_content_in_trajectory:
761	            # Use completions folder for raw LLM responses
762	            completions_dir = self.logs_dir / "completions"
763	            trajectory = self._convert_completions_to_trajectory(
764	                completions_dir, events_dir
765	            )
766	        else:
767	            # Use events folder for parsed tool calls
768	            trajectory = self._convert_events_to_trajectory(events_dir)
769	
770	        if not trajectory:
771	            self.logger.debug("Failed to convert to trajectory")
772	            return
773	
774	        trajectory_path = self.logs_dir / "trajectory.json"
775	        try:
776	            with open(trajectory_path, "w") as f:
777	                json.dump(trajectory.to_json_dict(), f, indent=2)
778	        except Exception as e:
779	            self.logger.debug(f"Failed to write trajectory file: {e}")
780	            return
781	
782	        # Populate context from trajectory Pydantic model
783	        if trajectory.final_metrics:
784	            metrics = trajectory.final_metrics
785	            context.cost_usd = metrics.total_cost_usd
786	            context.n_input_tokens = metrics.total_prompt_tokens or 0
787	            context.n_cache_tokens = metrics.total_cached_tokens or 0
788	            context.n_output_tokens = metrics.total_completion_tokens or 0
789	        else:
790	            self.logger.debug("No final_metrics found in trajectory")
791	
792	    @override
793	    async def install(self, environment: BaseEnvironment) -> None:
794	        await self.exec_as_root(
795	            environment,
796	            command="apt-get update && apt-get install -y curl git build-essential tmux",
797	            env={"DEBIAN_FRONTEND": "noninteractive"},
798	        )
799	        # Create /opt/openhands-venv owned by the agent user
800	        agent_user = environment.default_user or "root"
801	        await self.exec_as_root(
802	            environment,
803	            command=f"mkdir -p /opt/openhands-venv && chown {agent_user}:{agent_user} /opt/openhands-venv",
804	        )
805	        # Build install command with version logic
806	        if self._git_version:
807	            install_cmd = f"uv pip install git+https://github.com/All-Hands-AI/OpenHands.git@{self._git_version}"
808	        elif self._version:
809	            install_cmd = f"uv pip install openhands-ai=={self._version}"
810	        else:
811	            install_cmd = "uv pip install openhands-ai"
812	        await self.exec_as_agent(
813	            environment,
814	            command=(
815	                "set -euo pipefail; "
816	                "curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh && "
817	                'if [ -f "$HOME/.local/bin/env" ]; then source "$HOME/.local/bin/env"; fi && '
818	                f"uv python install {self._python_version} && "
819	                f"uv venv /opt/openhands-venv --python {self._python_version} && "
820	                "source /opt/openhands-venv/bin/activate && "
821	                "export SKIP_VSCODE_BUILD=true && "
822	                f"{install_cmd} && "
823	                "/opt/openhands-venv/bin/python -m openhands.core.main --version"
824	            ),
825	        )
826	
827	    def _build_mcp_config_toml(self) -> str | None:
828	        """Build OpenHands config.toml content with MCP server configuration.
829	
830	        Returns ``None`` when no MCP servers are configured.
831	        """
832	        if not self.mcp_servers:
833	            return None
834	
835	        sse_entries: list[str] = []
836	        shttp_entries: list[str] = []
837	        stdio_entries: list[str] = []
838	
839	        for server in self.mcp_servers:
840	            if server.transport == "sse":
841	                sse_entries.append(f'{{url = "{server.url}"}}')
842	            elif server.transport == "streamable-http":
843	                shttp_entries.append(f'{{url = "{server.url}"}}')
844	            elif server.transport == "stdio":
845	                args_str = ", ".join(f'"{a}"' for a in server.args)
846	                stdio_entries.append(
847	                    f'{{name = "{server.name}", command = "{server.command}", args = [{args_str}]}}'
848	                )
849	
850	        lines = ["[mcp]"]
851	        if sse_entries:
852	            lines.append(f"sse_servers = [{', '.join(sse_entries)}]")
853	        if shttp_entries:
854	            lines.append(f"shttp_servers = [{', '.join(shttp_entries)}]")
855	        if stdio_entries:
856	            lines.append(f"stdio_servers = [{', '.join(stdio_entries)}]")
857	
858	        return "\n".join(lines) + "\n"
859	
860	    @with_prompt_template
861	    async def run(
862	        self, instruction: str, environment: BaseEnvironment, context: AgentContext
863	    ) -> None:
864	        escaped_instruction = shlex.quote(instruction)
865	
866	        env = {}
867	
868	        is_hosted_vllm_model = (
869	            self.model_name is not None
870	            and self.model_name.lower().startswith("hosted_vllm/")
871	        )
872	
873	        # Handle LLM API key with fallback logic
874	        # For hosted_vllm models (local vLLM servers), use a dummy key if none set
875	        llm_api_key = self._get_env("LLM_API_KEY")
876	        if llm_api_key is not None:
877	            env["LLM_API_KEY"] = llm_api_key
878	        elif is_hosted_vllm_model:
879	            # Local vLLM servers don't validate API keys, use dummy
880	            env["LLM_API_KEY"] = "dummy-key-for-local-vllm"
881	        else:
882	            if self.model_name:
883	                try:
884	                    api_key_vars = get_api_key_var_names_from_model_name(
885	                        self.model_name
886	                    )
887	                    if len(api_key_vars) == 1:
888	                        api_key_var = api_key_vars[0]
889	                        api_key_value = self._get_env(api_key_var)
890	                        if api_key_value is not None:
891	                            env["LLM_API_KEY"] = api_key_value
892	                        else:
893	                            raise ValueError(
894	                                f"Unset API variable found for model {self.model_name}. "
895	                                f"Please set {api_key_var} or LLM_API_KEY environment variable"
896	                            )
897	                    else:
898	                        for api_key_var in api_key_vars:
899	                            api_key_value = self._get_env(api_key_var)
900	                            if api_key_value is not None:
901	                                # Use model-agnostic variables received by OpenHands
902	                                oh_api_key_var = api_key_var.replace("AZURE_", "LLM_")
903	                                env[oh_api_key_var] = api_key_value
904	                            else:
905	                                raise ValueError(
906	                                    f"Unset API variable found for model {self.model_name}. "
907	                                    f"Please set {api_key_var} or LLM_API_KEY environment variable"
908	                                )
909	                except ValueError as e:
910	                    raise ValueError(
911	                        f"Unable to determine API key for model {self.model_name}: {e}. "
912	                        "Please set LLM_API_KEY environment variable as fallback"
913	                    )
914	            else:
915	                raise ValueError(
916	                    "No LLM API key found and no model specified. "
917	                    "Please set LLM_API_KEY environment variable or specify a model"
918	                )
919	
920	        # Set model name
921	        if self.model_name:
922	            env["LLM_MODEL"] = self.model_name
923	        elif self._has_env("LLM_MODEL"):
924	            env["LLM_MODEL"] = self._get_env("LLM_MODEL")
925	        elif self._has_env("ANTHROPIC_MODEL"):
926	            env["LLM_MODEL"] = self._get_env("ANTHROPIC_MODEL")
927	        else:
928	            raise ValueError(
929	                "No LLM model found. Please set LLM_MODEL environment variable "
930	                "or specify it in the CLI"
931	            )
932	
933	        llm_base_url = self._get_env("LLM_BASE_URL")
934	        if llm_base_url is not None:
935	            env["LLM_BASE_URL"] = llm_base_url
936	        elif self._api_base:
937	            env["LLM_BASE_URL"] = self._api_base
938	        else:
939	            for candidate in (
940	                "HOSTED_VLLM_API_BASE",
941	                "VLLM_API_BASE",
942	                "OPENAI_API_BASE",
943	            ):
944	                candidate_value = self._get_env(candidate)
945	                if candidate_value is not None:
946	                    env["LLM_BASE_URL"] = candidate_value
947	                    break
948	
949	        llm_api_version = self._get_env("LLM_API_VERSION")
950	        if llm_api_version is not None:
951	            env["LLM_API_VERSION"] = llm_api_version
952	
953	        # Pass model_info token limits to OpenHands (critical for hosted_vllm models)
954	        # Without these, OpenHands uses LiteLLM defaults which may be too restrictive
955	        if self._model_info:
956	            if self._model_info.get("max_input_tokens"):
957	                env["LLM_MAX_INPUT_TOKENS"] = str(self._model_info["max_input_tokens"])
958	            if self._model_info.get("max_output_tokens"):
959	                env["LLM_MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS"] = str(
960	                    self._model_info["max_output_tokens"]
961	                )
962	
963	        # Merge declarative env vars (reasoning_effort, disable_tool_calls)
964	        env.update(self._resolved_env_vars)
965	
966	        # Disable browsing and prompt extensions
967	        env["AGENT_ENABLE_PROMPT_EXTENSIONS"] = "false"
968	        env["AGENT_ENABLE_BROWSING"] = "false"
969	        env["ENABLE_BROWSER"] = "false"
970	
971	        # Other sandbox settings
972	        env["SANDBOX_ENABLE_AUTO_LINT"] = "true"
973	        env["SKIP_DEPENDENCY_CHECK"] = "1"
974	        env["RUN_AS_OPENHANDS"] = "false"
975	        env["RUNTIME"] = "local"
976	        env["SU_TO_USER"] = "false"
977	
978	        # trajectory saving
979	        # note this trajectory is of openhands format, not ATIF. Also, it is only
980	        # saved at the end of the run, not during each step.
981	        env["SAVE_TRAJECTORY_PATH"] = "/logs/agent/openhands.trajectory.json"
982	
983	        # logging
984	        env["FILE_STORE"] = "local"
985	        env["FILE_STORE_PATH"] = "/logs/agent/"
986	        env["LLM_LOG_COMPLETIONS"] = "true"
987	        env["LLM_LOG_COMPLETIONS_FOLDER"] = "/logs/agent/completions/"
988	
989	        # Handle any other OpenHands environment variables from extra_env and os.environ
990	        for name, value in self._get_env_prefixed("OPENHANDS_").items():
991	            env[name] = value
992	
993	        # Write MCP config.toml if MCP servers are configured
994	        mcp_config = self._build_mcp_config_toml()
995	        config_file_path = "$HOME/.openhands/config.toml"
996	
997	        if mcp_config:
998	            escaped_config = shlex.quote(mcp_config)
999	            await self.exec_as_agent(
1000	                environment,
1001	                command=f"mkdir -p $HOME/.openhands && echo {escaped_config} > {config_file_path}",
1002	                env=env,  # ty: ignore[invalid-argument-type]
1003	            )
1004	
1005	        commands = [
1006	            # Use current directory as workspace
1007	            "SANDBOX_VOLUMES=${PWD}:/workspace:rw",
1008	            # Set user, in case agent runs as non-root
1009	            "USER=`id -un`",
1010	            "/opt/openhands-venv/bin/python -m openhands.core.main",
1011	            f"--task={escaped_instruction}",
1012	        ]
1013	
1014	        if mcp_config:
1015	            commands.append(f"--config-file={config_file_path}")
1016	
1017	        await self.exec_as_agent(
1018	            environment,
1019	            command=" ".join(commands)
1020	            + " 2>&1 </dev/null | stdbuf -oL tee /logs/agent/openhands.txt",
1021	            env=env,  # ty: ignore[invalid-argument-type]
1022	        )
1023
